title: When running rootless and passing in a non-1000 user ID with a workdir, the home directory becomes the workdir
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
when passing a non 1000 user ID into the user along with the `-w` work directory flag, the default home directory becomes what you passed in through the `-w` flag

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  The user ID is `1011`, the image's default user is `1000`
```bash
hysdsops@dustin-ThinkPad-T420:~$ id
uid=1011(hysdsops) gid=1011(hysdsops) groups=1011(hysdsops),27(sudo)
```

2. pass in the user is `-u 1011:1011` with `-w /usr/share`
```bash
hysdsops@dustin-ThinkPad-T420:~$ podman run -it \
>     --userns=keep-id \
>     -u 1011:1011 \
>     --entrypoint '' \
>     -v /data/work/jobs:/data/work/jobs \
>     -w /usr/share \
>     docker.io/hysds/pge-base:latest bash

bash-4.2$ pwd
/usr/share
bash-4.2$ echo $HOME 
/usr/share
```

**Describe the results you received:**
when passing in `-u 1011:1011` and `-w /usr/share` (just an example), the `$HOME` value becomes `/usr/share`
```bash
bash-4.2$ echo $HOME 
/usr/share
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
when passing in `-u 1011:1011` and `-w /usr/share`, the `$HOME` value becomes the original `$HOME` value: `/home/ops`
```bash
bash-4.2$ echo $HOME 
/home/ops
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```bash
$ podman version
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Wed Dec 31 16:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**



**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**
No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Does user 1011 exists in the container image?

Ok I tried this out and the 1011 user is not in the image, so podman generates a passwd entry for 1011 and sets the homedir to the workingdir.  
I don't see this as a bug.

@rhatdan the weird thing is it works normally when using docker
i'm trying to make the move from docker to podman but can't figure this out

```bash
[hysdsops@ip-##### ~]$ docker run -it \
>     -u 1011:1011 \
>     -v /data/work/jobs:/data/work/jobs \
>     -w /usr/share \
>     hysds/pge-base:develop bash

ops@ee7fbe41d96a:/usr/share$ echo $HOME 
/home/ops

ops@ee7fbe41d96a:/usr/share$ id
uid=1011(ops) gid=1011(ops) groups=1011(ops)
```
